sourceMovie.title: Hellraiser
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hellraiser (1987) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hereditary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hellraiser clicked for you, Hereditary (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Insidious: Chapter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hellraiser clicked for you, Insidious: Chapter 2 (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hellraiser?
faq.answer: Try Hereditary, Insidious: Chapter 2, Insidious: The Last Key—each shares DNA with Hellraiser while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hellraiser?
faq.answer: Hellraiser (1987) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hellraiser part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hellraiser Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
